rulings.Case Title: F. LEE LAWRENCE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 10, 2008, line 2, DEFENDANT DANA COMPANIES, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. THE DUNCAN DECLARATION LACKS SUFFICIENT FOUNDATIONAL FACTS TO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR THE COURT TO CONCLUDE THAT THE PRODUCTS USED BY PLAINTIFF DID NOT CONTAIN ASBESTOS. THE LACK OF FOUNDATIONAL FACTS IS SET FORTH IN PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION AT 2: 25 TO 3: 11 AND THE OBJECTIONS TO THE DUNCAN DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: DEBRA LEMMONS et al VS. JACKIE ROBINSON GARDEN APARTMENTS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 10, 2008, line 3, PLAINTIFF DEBRA LEMMONS MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUMMARY JUDGMENT ORDER IS DENIED. THE ISSUES OF LEGAL MALPRACTICE PRESENTED BY THIS CASE REQUIRED EXPERT TESTIMONY, PARTICULARLY GIVEN THE DISPUTE OVER THE APPLICABILITY OF THE RENT ORDINANCE. THE NEED FOR SUCH TESTIMONY IS WITHIN THE PURVIEW OF A LAWYER'S EXPERTISE. THIS IS NOT A MISTAKE CHARGEABLE TO A REASONABLY PRUDENT PERSON LACKING LEGAL TRAINING. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: HOUSING CORPORATION INC et al VS. DOUGLAS MCNIE et al

rulings.Case Title: RAY K SHAHANI VS. SCOTT MITCHELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue To Sonoma County
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 10, 2008, line 4, DEFENDANTS SCOTT MITCHELL, CLARK WOLF CONSULTING, LLC, CLARK WOLF, AND CLARK WOLF MEDIA LLC'S Motion For Change Of Venue To Sonoma County IS DENIED. VENUE IS PROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO BASED ON THE ALLEGATION THAT CONTRACTS WERE ENTERED INTO IN THIS COUNTY. DEFENDANT HAS NOT PRESENTED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO TRANSFER THE CASE BASED ON THE CONVENIENCE OF 3RD PARTY WITNESSES. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LAWSON-HAWKS INSURANCE AGENCY VS. DC RISK SOLUTIONS, INC. et al

rulings.Case Title: DEVINE COMMUNICATIONS, INCORPORATED A CALIFORNIA VS. PHILCONNET, FORM UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: For Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 10, 2008, line 8, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANT FILKONEK, NO OPPOSITION HAVING BEEN FILED. IT IS DENIED AS TO THE OTHER DEFENDANTS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO SEEK TO RENEW BASED ON ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE. AT THIS POINT, PLAINTIFF LACKS SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE OTHER DEFENDANTS MISAPPROPRIATED OR ATTEMPTED TO MISAPPROPRIATE PLAINTIFF'S TRADE SECRETS. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCANS FOR LIVABLE NEIGHBORHOODS, AN UNIN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn For Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Expenses, Request For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 10, 2008, line 9, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCANS FOR LIVABLE NEIGHBORHOODS Motion For Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Expenses. Plaintiff is entitled to an award of attorneys' fees under CCP section 1021. The fee application is supported by time records and declarations of experienced counsel in support of the work done and rates requested. Defendant has raised a number of objections and has presented evidence in support of its position. Having considered the parties' submissions, the Court makes the following award: Merits Susan Brandt-Hawley 17.8 hours at $500 per hour Paige Swartley 66.9 hours at $275 per hour Joseph Brecher 246.8 hours at $450 per hour Kathryn R. Devincenzi 858.1 hours at $425 Fee Application: Kathryn R. Devincenzi 90 hours at $425 per hour Administrative Record: Kathyrn R. Devincenzi 129.8 hours at $100 per hour Cost memorandum: Kathyrn R. Devincenzi 23.4 hours at $100 Writ: Kathyrn R. Devincenzi 10.5 hours at $425 Enhancement: Kathyrn R. Devincenzi is entitled to an enhancement of 1.3 for 758.1 uncompensated hours at a rate of $425. The award is based on the contingent risk, delay in payment, limitations on other work and the result achieved. The request for expenses is denied. Olson v. Automobile Club of So. Cal. (2008) 42 Cal.4th 1142. Plaintiff may file a cost bill to recover authorized costs. =(302/PJM/PB)
